System-wide minimum inventory level for bundle eligibility and alerts.
Status of the latest WebXlt validation (OK, QUARANTINED).
The Google Drive Folder ID for JLMops exports.
GA4 weekly export workbook (id) + report data-tab name. Read by StatusReportService.refreshKpiBlock. CONFIRM data_tab matches the actual GA4 add-on report tab name.

GA4 organic-traffic-by-audience workbook id, same workbook as ga4_report. Split into two single-param entries (see this + _tab below) because ConfigService.loadConfig only parses a second P03/P04 pair for schema.data.*/schema.log.* settings - a two-param system.* row like this used to be silently truncated to just the first pair (2026-07-02 finding, see .claude/bugs.md).

GA4 organic-traffic-by-audience report tab name, workbook id in ga4_audience_report_id. Dimensions: date, audienceName, sessionMedium (filtered to exactly 'organic'). Audiences present: All Users, HE IL, EN IL, Not IL, Purchasers - only HE IL/EN IL/Not IL are relevant (Not IL presumed English); All Users and Purchasers must be excluded from any sum, they are not additive with the language audiences.

GSC monthly export workbook (id) + data-tab name. Read by StatusReportService.refreshKpiBlock. data_tab is the literal sheet-tab name (Sheet1), not the workbook title — a prior value ('JLM GSC Weekly', the workbook's own title) never matched any real tab, so the reader silently fell back to Sheets()[0]. Sheet1 is the single stable tab going forward (2026-07-01) — the one extra dated tab the Search Analytics for Sheets add-on had created was removed; the add-on's recurring report must write its Date-dimensioned pull into Sheet1 itself, not spawn a new tab each run.
